# Air Icon Library

This site is the single source of truth for icons. If you are an AI coding
agent working on a project that references this library, use ONLY the icons
listed here — do not invent, generate, or substitute icons from elsewhere.

## Machine-readable index

GET /icons.json

Returns a JSON array of every icon. Each entry:

  {
    "name": "check_circle",           // stable identifier, snake_case
    "category": "Action",             // human-readable category
    "categorySlug": "action",         // used in the URL path
    "aliases": ["approve", "complete", "done", "validate", "verified", "yes"],
    "outlined": "/icons/outlined/action/check_circle.svg",
    "filled": "/icons/filled/action/check_circle.svg"
  }

`outlined` and `filled` are root-relative paths to static SVG files on this
same host. Prefix with this site's origin to get a fully-qualified, directly
usable URL, e.g.:

  https://<this-site>/icons/outlined/action/check_circle.svg

Each SVG is a plain 24x24 icon (`viewBox="0 0 24 24"`), monochrome
(`fill="black"`), with no external dependencies — safe to reference directly
via `<img src="...">`, fetch and inline, or download.

## Usage rules: Outlined vs. Filled

Straight from the design system's own guidance — pick a variant based on
*state*, not personal preference:

1. **Outlined is the default.** Use it for navigation, app bars, and content
   areas whenever the action or state is inactive. It's the clean,
   lightweight look every product should start from.
2. **Filled means active/selected.** Use it to show the current selection or
   location in navigational components — tabs, bottom navigation, sidebars.
   Apply one style consistently within a given component; never mix outlined
   and filled arbitrarily inside the same group (e.g. don't leave one item
   filled while its siblings are outlined for no reason).
3. **Filled can also be used for contrast**, independent of selection state —
   e.g. toasts and alerts use filled icons so they stay legible against
   varied background colors. Outside of that, retain whatever preset style
   the component or pattern you're working in already uses; don't override
   it icon-by-icon.

If a project/component doesn't clearly call for "active state" or "needs
contrast," default to outlined.

## Conventions

- Every icon exists in exactly two style variants: "outlined" and "filled".
- Icon names are unique only *within* a category — the same name can exist in
  two different categories with different meanings (e.g. "group" exists in
  both Format = combine/merge objects, and User = a group of people). Always
  resolve an icon by its full manifest entry (category + name), not by name
  alone, and always use the `outlined`/`filled` path exactly as given rather
  than constructing it by hand.
- Use `aliases` to match a requested concept (e.g. "close" or "x") to the
  correct icon name (e.g. "clear").

## Categories (405 icons total)

- Action (action): 81 icons
- Air (air): 13 icons
- Assets & Devices (assets-and-devices): 66 icons
- Climate (climate): 32 icons
- Connection (connection): 59 icons
- Date & Time (date-and-time): 29 icons
- Format (format): 53 icons
- Navigation (navigation): 51 icons
- Notifications (notifications): 11 icons
- User (user): 10 icons

## For humans

The homepage (/) is a searchable, browsable gallery of every icon in this
library — search by name, alias, or category, toggle outlined/filled, and
click any icon to copy its link or download the SVG.
